description: Letter from Shelomo b. Yehuda, Ramla, to Efrayim b. Shemarya (=Abū Kathīr Efrāyim b. Maḥfūẓ), Fustat. Dating: 1040 CE (after Kislev) per Gil and ca. 1025-1051 CE based on the years when Shelomo b. Yehuda served as Gaʾon. It is about a father who is constantly "complaining" (קובל בכל עת) to Shelomo b. Yehuda about his daughter, an aguna, who is constantly "screaming" (צועקת בכל עת) to be released from her condition. See Zinger's dissertation, pp. 319, 322.
tags: screaming, complaining, Arabic address, Ramla, Aguna, Shelomo b. Yehuda, Efrayim b. Shemarya
